source: Model answers (6 wines) + SAT readiness scale

readiness_reasoning_framework.principle: Readiness assessment must be consistent with development stage (nose) and structural characteristics (palate). Tannin, acidity, and presence of tertiary aromas are key signals.
scale_values: se puede beber ahora, pero tiene potencial para el envejecimiento
beber ahora: no adecuado para el envejecimiento o para un mayor envejecimiento

demasiado_joven.mentor_note: Use when wine needs more time; not just 'young' development

drink_now_has_potential.observed_in: Rustenberg Chardonnay, Ata Rangi Pinot Noir, Château Dutruch Bordeaux, Château Doisy-Daëne Barsac
drink_now_has_potential.mentor_note: The most common answer for premium wines in WSET L3 context

drink_now_no_ageing.observed_in: Ca di Alte Pinot Grigio (simple wine)
drink_now_no_ageing.mentor_note: Use for straightforward wines without ageing potential

demasiado_viejo.mentor_note: Rare in WSET exam wines as these are selected to demonstrate identifiable styles

consistency_rules: Quality 'excelente' + development 'evolucionado' → likely 'drink now but has potential' (still has life)

observed_model_answer_conclusions.readiness: drink now but has potential for ageing
drink now but has potential for ageing
drink now but has potential for ageing
drink now but has potential for ageing
